name: Audi

instructions.models: A4/A5, Q5 and above (where the menu option exists)
instructions.text: Switch off convenience-key unlocking in the MMI.
steps: In the MMI, go to Vehicle (or Settings) > Central locking.
Turn off "Open with convenience key" (also shown under Doors & Keys).
notes: On some models the car can still be locked by touching the handle pad; the temp method below also blocks unlocking.
Not all model years or trims expose this menu option.

instructions.text: Switch off keyless unlocking for the current lock cycle from the door handle.
steps: Press the lock button on the Advanced Key.
Within 5 seconds, touch the sensor on a door handle. Keyless unlocking is now off until you unlock with the key button.

info: Audi calls its keyless entry the "Advanced Key" (VW Group KESSY). Its infotainment is "MMI" (Multi Media Interface).
Advanced Key is an option across the Audi range.

name: Bentley

instructions.text: KESSY keyless unlocking can be switched off for the current lock cycle from the door handle.
steps: Lock the car by pressing the lock button on the key.
Within 5 seconds, touch the sensor on a door handle (do not grip it). Keyless unlocking is now off until you unlock with the key button.

info: Bentley uses the VW Group keyless system (Audi's "Advanced Key"/KESSY). Its infotainment is based on Audi's MMI.
Keyless entry is standard on the Bentayga, Continental GT and Flying Spur.

steps: Press the "lock" button on the digital key or remote control key fob.
notes: If the vehicle was locked with the remote control, it cannot be unlocked touchless.
If the vehicle has been idle for several days, touchless unlocking and locking is not possible until after the vehicle has been driven again.

name: Ford

instructions.models: Fiesta/Focus 2019 on; later Kuga, Puma and others
instructions.text: Ford's motion-sensor "sleeping" key fob deactivates the transmitter after the fob has been still for 40 seconds, so it cannot be picked up by a relay attack. It wakes the moment it is moved.
Switch off Intelligent Access (keyless entry) in the vehicle settings.
steps: On the information/message display (or SYNC touchscreen), open Settings, then Vehicle.
Select Intelligent Access and switch it off.
notes: You can still lock and unlock with the fob buttons.
Some model years and regional software do not offer this toggle; on those, rely on the sleeping key fob above.

info: Ford calls its keyless entry and push-button start "Intelligent Access". Its infotainment is "SYNC" (connected services: "FordPass").
Intelligent Access is available across much of the range on higher trims; the sleeping key fob was introduced partly to defeat relay theft.

name: Hyundai

instructions.text: Hyundai's owner's manual gives this as a way to prevent unintentional locking/unlocking (e.g. at a car wash). It stops the door-handle touch sensor responding.
steps: Press the lock button on the smart key.
Immediately press and hold the lock and unlock buttons together for more than 4 seconds.
The hazard warning lights blink 4 times and the door-handle touch sensor stops responding.
notes: Press the lock or unlock button on the fob to switch keyless entry back on.
There is no documented menu setting that fully disables smart-key touch entry; the "Welcome"/"Approach unlock" toggles only affect the welcome lights and mirrors.

info: Hyundai calls its keyless entry and push-button start the "Smart Key" system. Its latest infotainment is "ccNC" (connected car Navigation Cockpit); connected services are "Bluelink".
Smart Key is standard or optional across most current Hyundai models; the touch sensor works when the fob is within about 1 m of the handle.

steps: Open the driver's door (using the fob).
Place the key fob inside the car.
Press the headlamp button on the key fob 3 times.
Press the unlock button on the key fob once.
Take the keys outside the car.
Close the driver's door.
Lock the car using the fob.
Unlock the car using the fob.
notes: To re-enable it, follow the same procedure but press the lock button at step 4.
You must first unlock and then re-lock the car with the fob -- not the keyless button on the door handle.

name: Kia

instructions.text: Kia's owner's manual gives this as a way to prevent unintentional locking/unlocking. It stops the door-handle touch sensor responding.
steps: Press the lock button on the smart key.
Immediately press and hold the lock and unlock buttons together for more than 4 seconds.
The hazard warning lights blink 4 times and the door-handle touch sensor stops responding.
notes: Press the lock or unlock button on the fob to switch keyless entry back on.
There is no documented menu setting that fully disables smart-key touch entry; the fob method above is the way to switch it off.

info: Kia calls its keyless entry and push-button start the "Smart Key" system. Its latest infotainment is "ccNC" (connected car Navigation Cockpit); connected services are "Kia Connect".
Smart Key is standard or optional across most current Kia models; the touch sensor works when the fob is within about 1 m of the handle.

name: Lexus

instructions.text: Lexus uses Toyota's "battery-saving function": it stops the key receiving radio waves, which also defeats relay theft.
steps: Press and hold the "lock" button.
While still holding it, press the "unlock" button twice.
The light on the fob flashes 4 times to confirm keyless entry is now disabled.
notes: To turn keyless entry back on, press any button on the fob.

info: Lexus calls its keyless entry and push-button start the "Smart Key System". The infotainment is "Lexus Interface" (2022 on; older models: "Lexus Enform").
Keyless entry is standard across the Lexus range, and the fob uses the same battery-saving procedure as Toyota.

name: Mazda

instructions.text: Turning on the "key transmitter power saving function" switches off the advanced keyless entry and restricts the fob's battery use.
steps: Press the lock button on the transmitter 4 times within 3 seconds -- the operation indicator light turns on.
While the indicator light is on, press and hold the lock button for 1.5 seconds or longer.
Press any button on the transmitter to check the indicator light does not turn on or flash -- this confirms keyless entry is off.
notes: Repeat the same steps to switch keyless entry back on.
While power saving is on, start the engine by touching the key to the start/stop button (as you would with a flat key battery).

info: Mazda calls its keyless entry the "Advanced Keyless Entry System". Its infotainment is "Mazda Connect".
Advanced Keyless is fitted to higher trims across the range; lower trims use a conventional remote.

steps: Lock the car as usual.
Press the lock button again twice, quickly.
Lock the car using the button on the key fob.
notes: Once locked with the fob, the car cannot be opened by touching the handle until you unlock it with the fob again.

name: Nissan

instructions.text: Turning off the door request switch disables touch-the-handle keyless entry; you can still lock and unlock with the fob buttons.
steps: On the vehicle information display, open Settings.
Go to Locking | I-Key Door Lock.
Turn it off to deactivate the request switch on the door handles.

instructions.text: The Intelligent Key has a sleep function: if it is not physically moved for 2 minutes or more it stops transmitting, so a still key cannot be picked up by relay thieves.

info: Nissan calls its keyless system the "Intelligent Key" (sometimes "i-Key"). Its infotainment is "NissanConnect".
Intelligent Key is available across most of the current Nissan range; the door handles carry a request switch that unlocks the car when the key is nearby.

name: Peugeot

instructions.text: Switch off hands-free (keyless) access in the vehicle settings.
steps: Press the car-settings button (car symbol) to open the vehicle menu.
Open Comfort.
Set "Automatic hands-free access" (keyless entry) to Off.

instructions.text: On cars without the menu toggle, deactivate hands-free access using the key and the centre console.
steps: Press and hold the lock button on the key.
After about 3 seconds, also press the lock button on the centre console while still holding the key button.
After about 3 seconds, release the console button, then the key button. A beep confirms keyless entry is disabled.
notes: Repeat the same procedure to switch keyless entry back on.

info: Peugeot calls its keyless system "Keyless Entry and Start" (hands-free access). Its infotainment is "Peugeot i-Connect" within the i-Cockpit.
Hands-free access is an option on most current Peugeots (208, 2008, 308, 3008, 5008, 408).

name: Porsche

instructions.text: Porsche calls its keyless system "Entry & Drive". You can deactivate it on the key (per Porsche's Good to Know guide).
steps: Press and hold one button on the key while pressing the unlock button within 1 second.
Keep both buttons pressed until the indicator light on the key lights up continuously - this confirms Entry & Drive is deactivated.
notes: Pressing the key buttons again reactivates Entry & Drive.
Passive entry for the doors, front compartment and tailgate can also be de-selected in the PCM settings.

info: Porsche calls its keyless entry and start "Entry & Drive". Its infotainment is "PCM" (Porsche Communication Management).
Entry & Drive is an option across the range (911, Cayenne, Macan, Panamera, Taycan).

steps: With the key inside the car and the door open,
press the headlight button 3 times, then the unlock button.
Then lock the car with the fob as normal.

name: Skoda

instructions.text: KESSY keyless unlocking can be switched off for the current lock cycle from the door handle.
steps: Lock the car by pressing the lock button on the key.
Within 5 seconds, touch the sensor on a door handle with a finger (do not grip it). The indicators flash once to confirm.
Keyless unlocking is now off; to get in you must press the unlock button on the key.
notes: This resets the next time you unlock with the key, so repeat each time you park.

info: Skoda calls its keyless system "KESSY". Its infotainment is Skoda's system ("Columbus", "Amundsen" or "Bolero").
KESSY is an option across the range (Octavia, Superb, Kodiaq, Karoq, Enyaq).

name: Subaru

instructions.models: Models with Keyless Access and push-button start
instructions.text: Subaru lets you fully disable the keyless access function (proximity entry and push-button start) using the driver's door and power door lock switch. The fob buttons still lock and unlock the car.
steps: Sit in the driver's seat and close the door.
Push the rear (unlock) side of the power door lock switch.
Within 5 seconds, open the driver's door.
Within 5 seconds, push the unlock side of the switch twice while the door is open.
Within 30 seconds, close and open the driver's door twice.
Within 30 seconds, push the unlock side of the switch twice while the door is open.
Within 30 seconds, close and open the driver's door once.
Within 5 seconds, close the door. A chirp confirms the functions are disabled.
notes: In the two-press steps, press the switch slowly -- pressing too quickly can stop it working.
Perform the same procedure again to switch keyless access back on.

info: Subaru calls its keyless system "Keyless Access with push-button start". Its infotainment is "Subaru Starlink".
Keyless Access is fitted to higher trims; unlike most rivals it can be switched off completely using the procedure above.

steps: Press the "lock" button once.
On the centre touchscreen, go to the Settings tab.
Open the Doors & Locks menu.
Switch 'Passive Entry' OFF (you must press the brake pedal).

name: Toyota

instructions.models: Most models with Smart Key (push-button start)
instructions.text: Toyota's owner's manual calls this the "battery-saving function": it stops the key receiving radio waves, which also defeats relay theft.
steps: Press and hold the lock button on the key fob.
While still holding lock, press the unlock button twice.
The light on the fob flashes 4 times to confirm keyless entry is now disabled.
notes: To turn keyless entry back on, press any button on the fob.
Touching the door handle or pressing start will not work until you wake the fob.

info: Toyota calls its keyless entry and push-button start the "Smart Key System". The infotainment is "Toyota Audio Multimedia" (older models: "Entune").
Smart Key is standard or optional on most current Toyota models; entry-level trims often have a conventional remote without proximity unlocking.

name: Vauxhall

instructions.text: On current (Stellantis-platform) Vauxhalls, switch off hands-free (keyless) access in the vehicle settings.
steps: Open the vehicle/car settings menu on the touchscreen.
Open Comfort.
Set "Automatic hands-free access" (keyless entry) to Off.
notes: Older GM-based models (Insignia, Astra K) instead have a keyless setting in the on-screen Settings menu.

info: Vauxhall (Opel) calls its keyless system "Keyless Open & Start". Its infotainment is the Vauxhall Multimedia/Navi system (connected services: "Vauxhall Connect").
Keyless Open is an option on most current models (Corsa, Mokka, Astra, Grandland).

name: Volkswagen

instructions.text: KESSY keyless unlocking can be switched off for the current lock cycle from the door handle.
steps: Lock the car by pressing the lock button on the key.
Within 5 seconds, touch the sensor surface on a door handle with a finger (do not grip the handle). The indicators flash once to confirm.
Keyless unlocking is now off; to get in you must press the unlock button on the key.
notes: This resets the next time you unlock with the key, so repeat each time you park.

instructions.models: ID.3, ID.4 and other infotainment-equipped models
instructions.text: Keyless Access can be turned off permanently in the infotainment.
steps: On the infotainment screen, open the Vehicle menu.
Open the Keyless Access (door/locking) settings.
Turn Keyless Access off.

instructions.text: The key goes to sleep after about 15 minutes of inactivity, which also blocks relay attacks.

info: Volkswagen calls its keyless system "KESSY" (Keyless Entry, Start and exit System), marketed as "Keyless Access". Its infotainment is the Volkswagen infotainment system ("Discover Media/Pro" on older cars).
Keyless Access is an option across most of the range.

name: Chevrolet

instructions.text: Switch off passive (keyless) entry in the infotainment settings; the fob buttons still lock and unlock.
steps: On the infotainment screen, go to Settings | Vehicle | Remote Lock, Unlock, Start.
Turn Passive Door Unlock off so the handles no longer unlock when the fob is nearby.
Optionally turn Passive Locking off too (the walk-away auto-lock).

info: Chevrolet calls its keyless entry "Keyless Open and Start" (passive entry/start, PEPS). Its infotainment is the "Chevrolet Infotainment System" (formerly MyLink); connected services are "OnStar".
Keyless Open is available on most current Chevrolet models on higher trims.

name: GMC

instructions.text: Switch off passive (keyless) entry in the infotainment settings; the fob buttons still lock and unlock.
steps: On the infotainment screen, go to Settings | Vehicle | Remote Lock, Unlock, Start.
Turn Passive Door Unlock off so the handles no longer unlock when the fob is nearby.
Optionally turn Passive Locking off too (the walk-away auto-lock).

info: GMC calls its keyless entry "Keyless Open and Start" (passive entry/start). Its infotainment is the "GMC Infotainment System"; connected services are "OnStar".
Keyless Open is standard or optional across the GMC range.

name: Cadillac

instructions.text: Switch off passive (keyless) entry in the infotainment settings; the fob buttons still lock and unlock.
steps: On the infotainment screen, go to Settings | Vehicle | Remote Lock, Unlock, Start.
Turn Passive Door Unlock off so the handles no longer unlock when the fob is nearby.
Optionally turn Passive Locking off too (the walk-away auto-lock).

info: Cadillac calls its keyless entry "Keyless Open and Start" (passive entry/start). Its infotainment is the Cadillac user experience system (formerly "CUE"); connected services are "OnStar".
Keyless Open is standard across the Cadillac range.

name: Buick

instructions.text: Switch off passive (keyless) entry in the infotainment settings; the fob buttons still lock and unlock.
steps: On the infotainment screen, go to Settings | Vehicle | Remote Lock, Unlock, Start.
Turn Passive Door Unlock off so the handles no longer unlock when the fob is nearby.
Optionally turn Passive Locking off too (the walk-away auto-lock).

info: Buick calls its keyless entry "Keyless Open and Start" (passive entry/start). Its infotainment is the "Buick Infotainment System"; connected services are "OnStar".
Keyless Open is available across the Buick range on higher trims.

name: Jeep

instructions.text: "Passive Entry" (the touch-the-handle part of Keyless Enter-N-Go) can be switched off in Uconnect.
steps: On the Uconnect touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (menu wording varies by Uconnect version).
Turn Passive Entry off.
notes: Only touch-the-handle entry is switched off; the fob lock/unlock buttons keep working.

info: Jeep uses Stellantis "Keyless Enter-N-Go" (with Passive Entry). Its infotainment is "Uconnect".
Passive Entry is available across the Jeep range on higher trims.

name: Dodge

instructions.text: "Passive Entry" (the touch-the-handle part of Keyless Enter-N-Go) can be switched off in Uconnect.
steps: On the Uconnect touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (menu wording varies by Uconnect version).
Turn Passive Entry off.
notes: Only touch-the-handle entry is switched off; the fob lock/unlock buttons keep working.

info: Dodge uses Stellantis "Keyless Enter-N-Go" (with Passive Entry). Its infotainment is "Uconnect".
Passive Entry is available on Charger, Challenger, Durango and Hornet on higher trims.

name: Chrysler

instructions.text: "Passive Entry" (the touch-the-handle part of Keyless Enter-N-Go) can be switched off in Uconnect.
steps: On the Uconnect touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (menu wording varies by Uconnect version).
Turn Passive Entry off.
notes: Only touch-the-handle entry is switched off; the fob lock/unlock buttons keep working.

info: Chrysler uses Stellantis "Keyless Enter-N-Go" (with Passive Entry). Its infotainment is "Uconnect".
Passive Entry is available on the 300, Pacifica and Voyager on higher trims.

name: Ram

instructions.text: "Passive Entry" (the touch-the-handle part of Keyless Enter-N-Go) can be switched off in Uconnect.
steps: On the Uconnect touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (menu wording varies by Uconnect version).
Turn Passive Entry off.
notes: Only touch-the-handle entry is switched off; the fob lock/unlock buttons keep working.

info: Ram uses Stellantis "Keyless Enter-N-Go" (with Passive Entry). Its infotainment is "Uconnect".
Passive Entry is available on 1500/2500/3500 on higher trims.

name: Lincoln

instructions.text: Switch off Intelligent Access (keyless entry) in the vehicle settings; the fob buttons still lock and unlock.
steps: On the SYNC touchscreen, go to Settings | Vehicle | Locks.
Set Intelligent Access (keyless entry) to off.
On cars without a touchscreen, use the steering-wheel information display: Settings | Vehicle | Locks | Intelligent Access.
notes: Some model years and regional software do not offer this toggle.

info: Lincoln calls its keyless entry and push-button start "Intelligent Access". It shares Ford's "SYNC" infotainment (connected services: "Lincoln Connect").
Intelligent Access is standard across the Lincoln range.

name: Honda
info: Honda calls its keyless entry and push-button start "Smart Entry" (with Walk Away Auto Lock). Its infotainment and connected services are "HondaLink".
Honda's owner's manuals do not document a setting to fully switch off Smart Entry; the only related options are Walk Away Auto Lock and which doors unlock.

name: Acura
info: Acura calls its keyless entry the "Keyless Access System" (with Walk Away Auto Lock). Its infotainment is the "True Touchpad Interface"; connected services are "AcuraLink".
Like Honda, Acura does not document a setting to fully switch off keyless entry; the related options are Walk Away Auto Lock and auto door lock/unlock.

name: Infiniti

instructions.text: Infiniti shares Nissan's Intelligent Key. Turning off lock/unlock by the door-handle request switch disables touch-the-handle entry; the fob buttons still lock and unlock.
steps: In the vehicle settings menu (on the InTouch screen), open the door lock / Intelligent Key settings.
Turn off lock and unlock by the door-handle request switch.
notes: Like Nissan, the Intelligent Key fob also stops transmitting after about 2 minutes without movement.

info: Infiniti calls its keyless system the "Intelligent Key". Its infotainment and connected services are "INFINITI InTouch".
Intelligent Key is standard across the Infiniti range (Q50, Q60, QX50/55/60/80).

name: Genesis

instructions.text: Genesis shares Hyundai's smart key. The owner's manual gives this as a way to prevent unintentional locking/unlocking.
steps: Press the lock button on the smart key.
Immediately press and hold the lock and unlock buttons together for more than 4 seconds.
The hazard warning lights blink 4 times and the door-handle touch sensor stops responding.
notes: Press the lock or unlock button on the fob to switch keyless entry back on.

info: Genesis uses Hyundai's "Smart Key" system. Its infotainment has no separate brand name (referred to as the Genesis Display Audio / AVN system); connected services are "Genesis Connected Services".
Keyless entry is standard across the Genesis range.

name: Fiat

instructions.text: "Passive Entry" (touch-the-handle keyless entry) can be switched off in Uconnect on models that have it.
steps: On the Uconnect touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (menu wording varies by Uconnect version).
Turn Passive Entry off.
notes: Only touch-the-handle entry is switched off; the fob lock/unlock buttons keep working.

info: Fiat uses Stellantis "Keyless Entry/Go" (with Passive Entry) on Uconnect-equipped models such as the 500e, 600 and Tipo. Its infotainment is "Uconnect".
Small models without a Uconnect touchscreen may not offer the disable toggle.

name: Abarth

instructions.text: "Passive Entry" (touch-the-handle keyless entry) can be switched off in Uconnect on models that have it.
steps: On the Uconnect touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (menu wording varies by Uconnect version).
Turn Passive Entry off.
notes: Only touch-the-handle entry is switched off; the fob lock/unlock buttons keep working.

info: Abarth (Fiat's performance brand) uses Stellantis "Keyless Entry/Go" with Passive Entry on Uconnect-equipped models such as the 500e-based 595/695. Its infotainment is "Uconnect".
Older 500-based models without a Uconnect touchscreen may not offer the disable toggle.

name: Alfa Romeo

instructions.text: "Passive Entry" (touch-the-handle keyless entry) can be switched off in Uconnect.
steps: On the Uconnect touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (menu wording varies by Uconnect version).
Turn Passive Entry off.
notes: Only touch-the-handle entry is switched off; the fob lock/unlock buttons keep working.

info: Alfa Romeo uses Stellantis "Keyless Entry/Go" with Passive Entry. Its infotainment is "Uconnect".
Passive Entry can be switched off in Uconnect on Giulia, Stelvio, Tonale and Junior.

name: Maserati

instructions.text: Maserati's Passive Entry (touch-the-handle keyless entry) can be set on or off from the settings menu on models with the MIA system.
steps: On the Maserati Intelligent Assistant (MIA) screen, open the settings menu.
Find Passive Entry and set it to Off.
notes: On older Ghibli, Levante and Quattroporte the toggle was in the Uconnect menu; Maserati removed the disable option on some model years over lock-out concerns.

info: Maserati calls its keyless entry "Passive Entry". Its infotainment is the "Maserati Intelligent Assistant" (MIA, Android Automotive); older cars used "Maserati Touch Control Plus".
Keyless entry is standard across the Maserati range (Ghibli, Quattroporte, Levante, Grecale, MC20, GranTurismo).

name: Citroen

instructions.text: Switch off hands-free (keyless) access in the vehicle settings.
steps: Press the car-settings button to open the vehicle menu.
Open Comfort.
Set "Automatic hands-free access" (keyless entry) to Off.

instructions.models: C5 Aircross and similar without the menu option
instructions.text: On cars without the menu toggle, deactivate hands-free access using the key and the centre console.
steps: Press and hold the lock button on the key fob.
After about 3 seconds, also press the lock button on the centre console while still holding the fob button.
After about 3 seconds, release the console button, then the fob button. A beep confirms keyless entry is disabled.
notes: Repeat the same procedure to switch keyless entry back on.

info: Citroën calls its keyless system "Keyless Entry and Start" (hands-free access). Its infotainment is the "Citroën Connect" system.
Hands-free access is an option across the range (C3, C4, C5 Aircross, C5 X, ë-C4).

name: DS

instructions.text: DS shares Peugeot/Citroën's hands-free access. Switch off hands-free (keyless) access in the vehicle settings.
steps: Open the vehicle/car settings menu on the touchscreen.
Open Comfort.
Set "Automatic hands-free access" (keyless entry) to Off.
notes: On models without the menu toggle, hold the key lock button and the centre-console lock button together for a few seconds until a beep confirms keyless entry is off; repeat to re-enable.

info: DS Automobiles calls its keyless system "Keyless Entry and Start" (hands-free access). Its infotainment is the "DS IRIS" system.
Hands-free access is available across the DS range (DS 3, DS 4, DS 7, DS 9).

name: SEAT

instructions.text: KESSY keyless unlocking can be switched off for the current lock cycle from the door handle.
steps: Lock the car by pressing the lock button on the key.
Within 5 seconds, touch the sensor on a door handle with a finger (do not grip it). The indicators flash once to confirm.
Keyless unlocking is now off; to get in you must press the unlock button on the key.
notes: This resets the next time you unlock with the key. The procedure is shared across the VW Group (KESSY).

info: SEAT calls its keyless system "Kessy". Its infotainment is the "SEAT Media System".
Kessy is an option across the range (Ibiza, Leon, Arona, Ateca, Tarraco).

name: Cupra

instructions.text: KESSY keyless unlocking can be switched off for the current lock cycle from the door handle.
steps: Lock the car by pressing the lock button on the key.
Within 5 seconds, touch the sensor on a door handle with a finger (do not grip it). The indicators flash once to confirm.
Keyless unlocking is now off; to get in you must press the unlock button on the key.
notes: This resets the next time you unlock with the key. The procedure is shared across the VW Group (KESSY).

info: Cupra (SEAT's performance brand) uses the VW Group "Kessy" keyless system. Its infotainment is the Cupra/SEAT Media System.
Kessy is fitted across the range (Formentor, Born, Leon, Ateca, Tavascan).

steps: Lock the car using the button on the key fob.
notes: Once locked with the fob, the car cannot be opened by touching the handle until you unlock it with the fob again.

name: Lamborghini

instructions.text: KESSY keyless unlocking can be switched off for the current lock cycle from the door handle.
steps: Lock the car by pressing the lock button on the key.
Within 5 seconds, touch the sensor on a door handle (do not grip it). Keyless unlocking is now off until you unlock with the key button.

info: The Lamborghini Urus shares the VW Group keyless system (Audi's "Advanced Key"/KESSY) and Audi's MMI infotainment.
Keyless entry applies to the Urus; the Huracan and Revuelto supercars use a conventional remote.
